audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for resurrection morning, Mary witnesses, angel/messenger, earthquake, empty tomb, Jesus' greeting, guard report/bribe, Galilee meeting, authority, discipling nations, baptismal formula, teaching/keeping, and presence to completion of age.

verses.restored: After the Sabbath, as the first day of the week was dawning, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb.
And look, there was a great earthquake, for a messenger of the Lord descended from heaven, came, rolled away the stone, and sat upon it.
His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.
From fear of him the guards trembled and became like dead ones.
The messenger answered the women, Do not fear, for I know that you seek Jesus, the crucified one.
He is not here, for he was raised, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.
Go quickly and tell his disciples that he was raised from the dead; and look, he goes before you to Galilee. There you will see him. Look, I have told you.
Departing quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to report to his disciples.
And look, Jesus met them, saying, Rejoice. They came, took hold of his feet, and bowed/prostrated before him.
Then Jesus said to them, Do not fear. Go report to my brothers and sisters that they should go away to Galilee, and there they will see me.
While they were going, look, some of the guard went into the city and reported to the chief priests all that had happened.
After gathering with the elders and taking counsel, they gave sufficient silver to the soldiers,
saying, Say that his disciples came by night and stole him while we slept.
If this is heard by the governor, we will persuade him and keep you free from trouble.
They took the silver and did as they were taught. This word was spread among Judeans until this day.
The eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
When they saw him, they bowed/prostrated, but some doubted/divided.
Jesus came near and spoke to them, saying, All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
Therefore go, disciple all the nations, baptizing them into the name of the Father and of the Son and of the holy Spirit,
teaching them to keep all that I commanded you. And look, I am with you all the days until the completion of the age.
